heading: Staff qualifications and development
label: 9843a Staff qualifications and development

children.label: A
children.text: planning and implementing learning experiences that advance the intellectual and physical development of children, including improving the readiness of children for school by developing their literacy, phonemic, and print awareness, their understanding and use of language, their understanding and use of increasingly complex and varied vocabulary, their appreciation of books, their understanding of early math and early science, their problem-solving abilities, and their approaches to learning;

children.label: B
children.text: establishing and maintaining a safe, healthy learning environment;

children.label: C
children.text: supporting the social and emotional development of children; and

children.label: D
children.text: encouraging the involvement of the families of the children in a Head Start program and supporting the development of relationships between children and their families.

children.label: i
children.text: a baccalaureate or advanced degree in early childhood education; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a baccalaureate or advanced degree and coursework equivalent to a major relating to early childhood education, with experience teaching preschool-age children.

children.label: I
children.text: have the capacity to offer assistance to other teachers in the implementation and adaptation of curricula to the group and individual needs of children in a Head Start classroom; and

children.label: aa
children.text: a baccalaureate or advanced degree in early childhood education; or

children.label: bb
children.text: a baccalaureate or advanced degree and coursework equivalent to a major relating to early childhood education, with experience teaching preschool-age children; and

children.label: I
children.text: at least a child development associate credential;

children.label: II
children.text: enrolled in a program leading to an associate or baccalaureate degree; or

children.label: III
children.text: enrolled in a child development associate credential program to be completed within 2 years.

children.label: aa
children.text: describe continuing progress each year toward achieving the goals described in subparagraphs (A) and (B); and

children.label: bb
children.text: annually submit to the Secretary a report indicating the number and percentage of classroom personnel described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) in center-based programs with child development associate credentials or associate, baccalaureate, or advanced degrees;

children.label: II
children.text: compile and submit a summary of all program reports described in subclause (I)(bb) to the Committee on Education and Labor of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate; and

children.label: III
children.text: not impose any penalties or sanctions on any individual Head Start agency, program, or staff in the monitoring of local agencies and programs under this subchapter not meeting the requirements of subparagraph (A) or (B).

children.heading: Construction
children.label: D Construction
children.text: In this paragraph a reference to a Head Start agency, or its program, services, facility, or personnel, shall not be considered to be a reference to an Early Head Start agency, or its program, services, facility, or personnel.

children.label: i
children.text: a child development associate credential that is appropriate to the age of children being served in center-based programs;

children.label: ii
children.text: a State-awarded certificate for preschool teachers that meets or exceeds the requirements for a child development associate credential;

children.label: iii
children.text: an associate degree in early childhood education;

children.label: iv
children.text: an associate degree in a related field and coursework equivalent to a major relating to early childhood education, with experience teaching preschool-age children; or

children.label: v
children.text: a baccalaureate degree and has been admitted into the Teach For America program, passed a rigorous early childhood content exam, such as the Praxis II, participated in a Teach For America summer training institute that includes teaching preschool children, and is receiving ongoing professional development and support from Teach For America’s professional staff.

children.label: i
children.text: an associate degree in early childhood education;

children.label: ii
children.text: an associate degree in a related field and coursework equivalent to a major relating to early childhood education, with experience teaching preschool-age children; or

children.label: iii
children.text: a baccalaureate degree and has been admitted into the Teach For America program, passed a rigorous early childhood content exam, such as the Praxis II, participated in a Teach For America summer training institute that includes teaching preschool children, and is receiving ongoing professional development and support from Teach For America’s professional staff.

children.label: i
children.text: is enrolled in a program that grants a credential, certificate, or degree described in clauses (i) through (iv) of paragraph (3)(A); and

children.label: ii
children.text: will receive such credential, certificate, or degree under the terms of such program not later than 180 days after beginning employment as a teacher with such agency; and

children.label: i
children.text: the agency has attempted unsuccessfully to recruit an individual who has the qualifications described in clause (i) or (ii) of such paragraph, with respect to an individual who is enrolled in a program that grants a degree described in clause (i) or (ii) of such paragraph and will receive such degree in a reasonable time; and

children.label: I
children.text: a child development associate credential that is appropriate to the age of children being served in center-based programs; or

children.label: II
children.text: a State-awarded certificate for preschool teachers that meets or exceeds the requirements for a child development associate credential.

children.heading: Teacher in-service requirement
children.label: 5 Teacher in-service requirement
children.text: Each Head Start teacher shall attend not less than 15 clock hours of professional development per year. Such professional development shall be high-quality, sustained, intensive, and classroom-focused in order to have a positive and lasting impact on classroom instruction and the teacher’s performance in the classroom, and regularly evaluated by the program for effectiveness.

children.label: A
children.text: teach or work in a Head Start program for a minimum of 3 years after receiving the degree; or

children.label: B
children.text: repay the total or a prorated amount of the financial assistance received based on the length of service completed after receiving the degree.

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: 7 Use of funds
children.text: The Secretary shall require that any Federal funds provided directly or indirectly to comply with paragraph (2)(A) shall be used toward degrees awarded by an institution of higher education, as defined by section 1001 or 1002 of title 20.

children.heading: “Mentor teacher” defined; function
children.label: 1 “Mentor teacher” defined; function
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “mentor teacher” means an individual responsible for observing and assessing the classroom activities of a Head Start program and providing on-the-job guidance and training to the Head Start program staff and volunteers, in order to improve the qualifications and training of classroom staff, to maintain high quality education services, and to promote career development, in Head Start programs.

children.label: A
children.text: provide technical assistance and training to enable Head Start agencies to establish such positions;

children.label: B
children.text: give priority consideration, in providing assistance pursuant to subparagraph (A), to Head Start programs that have substantial numbers of new classroom staff or that are experiencing difficulty in meeting applicable education standards;

children.label: C
children.text: encourage Head Start programs to give priority consideration for such positions to Head Start teachers at the appropriate level of career advancement in such programs; and

children.label: D
children.text: promote the development of model curricula, designed to ensure the attainment of appropriate competencies of mentor teachers in Head Start programs.
review, and, as necessary, revise or develop new qualification standards for Head Start staff providing such services;
review, and as necessary, revise or develop maximum caseload requirements, as suggested by best practices;

promote the development of model curricula (on subjects including parenting training and family literacy) designed to ensure the attainment of appropriate competencies by individuals working or planning to work in the field of early childhood and family services;
promote the establishment of a credential that indicates attainment of the competencies and that is accepted nationwide; and
promote the use of appropriate strategies to meet the needs of special populations (including populations of limited English proficient children).

children.heading: Authority
children.label: 1 Authority
children.text: The Secretary may establish a program of fellowships, to be known as “Head Start Fellowships”, in accordance with this subsection. The Secretary may award the fellowships to individuals, to be known as “Head Start Fellows”, who are staff in local Head Start programs or other individuals working in the field of child development and family services.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The fellowship program established under this subsection shall be designed to enhance the ability of Head Start Fellows to make significant contributions to programs authorized under this subchapter, by providing opportunities to expand their knowledge and experience through exposure to activities, issues, resources, and new approaches, in the field of child development and family services.

children.label: i
children.text: in agencies of the Department of Health and Human Services administering programs authorized under this subchapter (in national or regional offices of such agencies);

children.label: ii
children.text: in local Head Start agencies and programs;

children.label: iii
children.text: in institutions of higher education;

children.label: iv
children.text: in public or private entities and organizations concerned with services to children and families; and

children.label: v
children.text: in other appropriate settings.

children.heading: Limitation for Fellows other than Head Start employees
children.label: B Limitation for Fellows other than Head Start employees
children.text: A Head Start Fellow who is not an employee of a local Head Start agency or program may be placed only in a fellowship position located in an agency or program specified in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: No placement in lobbying organizations
children.label: C No placement in lobbying organizations
children.text: Head Start Fellowship positions may not be located in any agency (including a center) whose primary purpose, or one of whose major purposes, is to influence Federal, State, or local legislation.

children.heading: Selection of Fellows
children.label: 4 Selection of Fellows
children.text: Head Start Fellowships shall be awarded on a competitive basis to individuals (other than Federal employees) selected from among applicants who are working, on the date of application, in local Head Start programs or otherwise working in the field of child development and children and family services.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 5 Duration
children.text: Head Start Fellowships shall be for terms of 1 year, and may be renewed for a term of 1 additional year.

children.heading: Authorized expenditures
children.label: 6 Authorized expenditures
children.text: From amounts made available under , the Secretary is authorized to make expenditures of not to exceed $1,000,000 for any fiscal year, for stipends and other reasonable expenses of the fellowship program.section 9835(a)(2)(E) of this title

children.heading: Status of Fellows
children.label: 7 Status of Fellows
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, Head Start Fellows shall not be considered to be employees or otherwise in the service or employment of the Federal Government. Head Start Fellows shall be considered to be employees for purposes of compensation for injuries under chapter 81 of title 5. Head Start Fellows assigned to positions located in agencies specified in paragraph (3)(A)(i) shall be considered employees in the executive branch of the Federal Government for the purposes of chapter 11 of title 18 and for purposes of any administrative standards of conduct applicable to the employees of the agency to which they are assigned.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 8 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate regulations to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Model staffing plans
children.label: e Model staffing plans
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary, in consultation with appropriate public agencies, private agencies, and organizations and with individuals with expertise in the field of children and family services, shall develop model staffing plans to provide guidance to local Head Start agencies and programs on the numbers, types, responsibilities, and qualifications of staff required to operate a Head Start program.May 18, 1994

children.heading: Professional development plans
children.label: f Professional development plans
children.text: Each Head Start agency and program shall create, in consultation with an employee, a professional development plan for all full-time Head Start employees who provide direct services to children and shall ensure that such plans are regularly evaluated for their impact on teacher and staff effectiveness. The agency and the employee shall implement the plan to the extent feasible and practicable.

children.heading: Staff recruitment and selection procedures
children.label: g Staff recruitment and selection procedures

children.text: conduct an interview of such individual;
verify the personal and employment references provided by such individual; and

children.label: A
children.text: a State, tribal, or Federal criminal record check covering all jurisdictions where the grantee provides Head Start services to children;

children.label: B
children.text: a State, tribal, or Federal criminal record check as required by the law of the jurisdiction where the grantee provides Head Start services; or

children.label: C
children.text: a criminal record check as otherwise required by Federal law.
